title: Entryway Bench and Storage Shelf with Hooks
summary: A two-piece mudroom set — a cubby bench to sit and stash shoes, plus a matching hook-and-shelf that mounts above it. Built from 1-by boards.
description:
This is the classic drop-zone combo: a bench with open cubbies for shoes and bags below, and a wall-mounted shelf with hooks above for coats and keys. Building them as two pieces is smart — the bench is structural and gets screwed together, while the shelf is light and can be brad-nailed, and mounting them separately lets you set the hook height to whoever actually uses it.

The bench is built from 1x12s, which the source notes honestly come out shallow — fine for a mudroom perch, but if you want a roomier seat, swap every 1x12 for a wider board (a 16" project panel) and keep the same layout. Because people sit on it, join the main boards with screws or pocket holes, not just nails.

The two details that make it look finished: cut the shelf's side pieces at an angle at the top so there's no sharp corner at eye level, and mount the shelf by screwing through the thick 1x8 hook board into a wall stud — a shelf loaded with heavy winter coats will pull anchors out of drywall. Find the stud.

materials.name: 1x12, 8 ft
materials.note: Bench top and bottom. Swap for a 16" project panel for a deeper seat.

materials.name: 1/4" plywood, 48" x 24"
materials.note: Backs for both the bench and the shelf.

cutList.note: Cut top at an angle to avoid a sharp corner at eye level.

steps.title: Cut the parts for both pieces
steps.body: From the 8 ft 1x12 cut the bench top at 48" and the bench bottom at 46-1/2"; from the 6 ft 1x12 cut two bench sides at 17-1/4" and two bench dividers at 13-1/4" — every 1x12 part is the board's full 11-1/4" width. From the two 8 ft 1x4 cut one bench top back trim at 48", two top side trim at 12", two footer sides at 11-1/4" and one footer front at 49-1/2"; those four "cut to fit" pieces are worth running long and trimming once the box is together. From the 10 ft 1x8 cut both 46-1/2" shelf main shelf/back pieces plus one 16-1/2" side and one 8-1/2" divider, which uses 118" of the 120"; from the 8 ft 1x8 cut the 49-1/2" shelf top front, the second 16-1/2" side and the second 8-1/2" divider. Cut the 1x2 shelf top back at 49-1/2". Last, split the 48" x 24" plywood into a 14" x 48" bench back and a 10" x 48" shelf back — 14" and 10" is the sheet's full 24" width with nothing left for the kerf, so cut the bench back to size first and let the shelf back finish about 1/8" narrow, where it is hidden.

steps.title: Build the bench box
steps.body: The two 17-1/4" sides run the full height and the 46-1/2" bottom fits between them, making the carcass 48" wide outside — the same as the 48" bench top, so the top lands flush at both ends. Because people sit on this, join the 1x12s with 2" screws or 2" wood screws set for 3/4" stock, not brads alone. Screw the sides to the bottom first, then stand the two 13-1/4" dividers on the bottom between them to split the run into three cubbies; the 13-1/4" divider is the clear cubby height, which puts the underside of the bottom about 3-1/4" up from the ends of the sides — right where the 3-1/2" footer trim will cover it. Check both diagonals for square before the seat goes anywhere near it.

steps.title: Add the seat and trim
steps.body: Attach the seat top to the base with 2" screws or pocket holes. Measure and cut the 1x4 trim to fit — top sides, footer sides, and footer front — and attach all with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue. If the center footer is hard to attach to the bottom, add a scrap 1x4 block flat-nailed to the underside as a backer.

steps.title: Back the bench
steps.body: Cut the bench back with a circular saw and save the scrap for the shelf back. Attach with 3/4" brad nails and glue.

steps.title: Build the shelf box
steps.body: The shelf is built the same way as the bench: the two 16-1/2" sides run the full height and the two 46-1/2" pieces — the main shelf and the hook board behind it — fit between them, giving the same 48" outside width so the two pieces line up on the wall. Before assembling, cut the top of each side at an angle so there is no square corner at eye level; the plan does not specify the angle, so pick one that looks right and cut both sides together, or use the first as a template — matching matters more than the number. Assemble with wood glue and 1-1/4" brad nails, since this piece hangs rather than gets sat on, setting the two 8-1/2" dividers between the shelf and the hook board to break the run into three bays like the bench below. Check the diagonals before the glue sets; the 1/4" back added next will lock in whatever shape you leave.

steps.title: Finish the shelf and add the top
steps.body: Attach the 49-1/2" 1x2 shelf top back and the 49-1/2" 1x8 top front with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue. Both run 1-1/2" past the 48" box, so set each to overhang 3/4" at either end and the ends will line up with each other automatically. Then attach the 10" x 48" plywood back with 3/4" brad nails and glue, catching both dividers as well as the sides — it is what keeps the light nailed box from racking once coats hang off it.

steps.title: Mount hooks and hang, then finish
steps.body: Attach the double hooks to the thick hook board. To hang the shelf, locate a wall stud and screw through the 1x8 hook board into the stud — a shelf full of heavy coats will pull drywall anchors out. Sand and finish both pieces to match.

images.alt: An entryway set with a cubby bench below and a hook-and-shelf coat organizer above
